mentions.note: New CEO Greg Abel debuts tomorrow; Cramer expects low-key style shift from Buffett; listening for wisdom; thinks Abel a business person focused on making money.
Large oil holdings (Occidental, Chevron) should benefit from rally but stock lagging; Warren Buffett premium evaporated with leadership transition; market not recognizing oil tailwind.
Down 12% since Buffett retirement; wait one year before judgment on new leadership. Hold position; need to see if philanthropic selling becomes problem.
Underperformed S&P by ~45% past 12 months, but CEO Greg Abel has strong hand; assets second to none; hold it — mistake to leave now.

Warren Buffett stepping down will bore holders; insurance/reinsurance/oil-gas/railroad mosaic intact. Perceived as reliant on Buffett's mystique rather than autonomous growth; holders may retire with him.

Caller asked about using Berkshire B for 20-30 year inheritance; Cramer advised against single-stock concentration despite admiration for Buffett—recommend diversified index fund instead to avoid single-name risk.
